meta.school: UC San Diego — Jacobs School of Engineering
meta.source: UCSD ECE M.S. / Ph.D. Degree Planners (research-areas), 2025-2026 & 2026-2027
meta.note: Organised by UCSD graduate school → department → program (grad.ucsd.edu/admissions/programs.html). Electrical & Computer Engineering has full course-level worksheets for all 13 research areas; every other department shows its verified degree types and a link to official requirements. Reference only — confirm with the department and the official requirements.

schools.name: School of Computing, Information and Data Sciences
Herbert Wertheim School of Public Health

overview.notes: Terminal coursework MA: nine 4-unit courses (36 units) + oral exam; PhD in seven concentrations.
MFA in Writing = 72 units; MA awarded only en route to the PhD.
PhD/DMA focused; MA earned only during doctoral progress (no terminal MA).
No terminal master's; MA earned only during PhD progress.
MFA (acting, dance, design, directing, playwriting, stage mgmt) ≥90 quarter-units; PhD joint with UC Irvine.
MFA = 72 units; PhD in Art History, Theory & Criticism (en-route non-terminal MA).
PhD plus contiguous BS/MS; MS ≥36 units of grad coursework + thesis.
MS in Data Science: 48 units (twelve 4-unit courses) + Plan I thesis or Plan II comprehensive.
Fully online Master of Data Science: 40 units (ten 4-unit courses incl. capstone), no thesis.
MAS in Data Science & Engineering: 38-unit coursework degree (11 courses) with capstone.
Professional master's (MIA/MPP/MCEPA/MAS-IA) coursework-based; PhD in Political Science & International Affairs.
SDSU/UCSD joint 4-year professional Doctor of Audiology; no coursework MS.
Research PhD (plus MD/PhD, PharmD/PhD); no standalone coursework master's.
SDSU/UCSD joint doctoral program (APA-accredited); no coursework MS.
Master of Advanced Studies, 40 units (34 required course units).
Skaggs MS: self-supporting 36-unit coursework degree + comprehensive exam.

departments.name: Interdisciplinary Research on Substance Use (JDP with SDSU)

overview.notes: SDSU/UCSD joint research doctoral program; no coursework MS.

departments.name: Language and Communicative Disorders (JDP with SDSU)

overview.notes: SDSU/UCSD joint doctoral program; interdisciplinary 5-year research degree.
Research PhD (MD/PhD option, computational neuroscience specialization); no coursework MS.
New Master of Advanced Studies (36 units), launching Fall 2026.
MS ≥60 units (36 biostat core + 16 advanced + 8 life sciences + electives); PhD also offered.
MPH: 64 units (36 core + 16 concentration + 12 electives); PhD & SDSU/UCSD JDP also offered.
PhD only (Astronomy & Astrophysics); ~10 grad courses; no standalone coursework MS.
MS Chemistry: Plan I thesis / Plan II comprehensive, min 36 units; separate BIOC MS/PhD.
MA (pure/applied) and MS (statistics) each 48 units of coursework.
PhD (Physics & Biophysics) primary; MS via Plan I/II, mainly en route to PhD.
MBAs plus specialized master's (Business Analytics, Quantitative Finance, Professional Accountancy) and a PhD in Management.
MS (Oceanography/Marine Biology/Earth Sciences) min 36 units (24 coursework + 12 research) + thesis; also MAS and PhD tracks.
PhD program; MA earned en route to the doctorate, not a terminal coursework degree.
PhD; MS only as an intermediate credential, not a terminal coursework degree.
PhD only; no terminal master's.
Terminal one-year coursework MS (bootcamp, core, electives, capstone); total units not published.
PhD only; no terminal coursework master's.
PhD & EdD plus M.Ed. credential and MA programs (credential/cohort based).
PhD; MA earned en route, not terminal.
Terminal one-year coursework MA: 36 units / 9 courses (6 core + 3 electives) + comprehensive exam & portfolio.
Terminal coursework MA: 38 units (42 with concentration); core seminar + theory + methods + electives + research.
PhD; MA and C.Phil. only as milestones en route, not terminal.
PhD only; joint doctoral program with SDSU.
PhD only; no MA program listed.
PhD; MA optional, earned during PhD study, not terminal.
PhD only; MA incidental en route, no terminal master's.
Professional Master of Urban & Regional Planning (72 units) + separate Urban Planning PhD.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive Exam
plans.note: 48 units total. At least 16 units must be 201+ ECE courses counting toward the degree. No more than 4 units of research (299) may count as technical electives. 3.0 GPA; complete within six quarters.

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 52 units total (the 4 units above the Plan II coursework are thesis / research units, e.g. ECE 299). At least 12 units must be 201+ ECE courses. No more than 8 units of research may count as technical electives. 3.0 GPA; complete within six quarters.

commonRules: All courses counted toward the degree must be taken for a letter grade and for 4 units, with the exception of research units.
Students CANNOT repeat a course unless they earned a D, F, or U grade.
Must meet the Academic Residency requirement.
Overall GPA of 3.0 or higher is required; the program must be completed within the six-quarter M.S. time limit.
Seminar courses cannot count toward the degree.

techElective.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, DSC, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH, PHYS or COGS taken for a letter grade may count. Exceptions require departmental approval.
extras: Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE coursework (ECE 111+ only, excluding 195/197/198/199/210/298) OR up to two 4-unit upper-div ECE courses plus one 4-unit CSE course (CSE 100+ only, excluding 123/140/140L/143/294) may be counted.
Research that may count: ECE 299, CSE 293/298/299, MAE 299, BENG 299, NANO 299, SE 299, DSC 299 (subject to the plan's research cap).

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: All four required (4 units each). MAE 280A may substitute for ECE 272A — credit toward the degree may be earned for only one of these.

courses.title: Dynamic Systems I: Linear Systems
courses.note: MAE 280A may substitute (credit for only one).

groups.name: Additional Units
groups.rule: Select 12 units (three 4-unit courses) from the list below. Multiple ECE 285 courses may count if each topic is different. MAE 280A cannot count here if ECE 272A was taken.

courses.title: Special Topics in Signal & Image Processing / Robotics & Control
Principles of AI: Probabilistic Reasoning & Learning
Linear Systems Theory
courses.note: Cannot count here if ECE 272A was taken.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: All three required (4 units each).

groups.name: Additional Units
groups.rule: Select 16 units (four 4-unit courses) from the list below.

courses.title: Optimization & Acceleration of Deep Learning on Hardware

courses.title: Special Topic in CE: Mobile Health Design

groups.name: Technical Electives
groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH or PHYS (letter grade). Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE/CSE coursework (ECE 111+ and CSE 100+) may count.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: All four required (4 units each).

groups.name: Additional Units
groups.rule: Select 16 units (four 4-unit courses) from the list below. ECE 285 may be taken multiple times if each topic is different.

courses.title: Universal Probability & Its Application in Data Science

groups.name: Technical Electives
groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, DSC, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH, PHYS or COGS (letter grade). Recommended: MATH 245A-B-C, MATH 282A-B, MATH 289C, COGS 260. Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: All four required (4 units each). No course substitutions allowed. ECE 251A is strongly recommended in your first year.

courses.title: Digital Signal Processing I
courses.note: Recommended in your first year; foundational.

groups.name: Additional Units
groups.rule: Select 12 units (three 4-unit courses) from the list below. ECE 250 is recommended for Ph.D. students.

groups.name: Technical Electives
groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ ECE course (letter grade), PLUS at most 4 units of 200+ courses from CSE, DSC, MAE, BENG, CENG, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH, PHYS or CogSci. Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

groups.name: Core Coursework — choose one sequence
groups.rule: Choose one sequence (12 units): ECE 264A-B plus a 3rd from 264C/264D/265A/265B/265C/265D/260A/260B; OR ECE 265A-B plus a 3rd from 265C/265D/264A/264B/264C/264D/260A/260B. Take A-B in order — a B- or higher is required to advance (and in ECE 264A & ECE 250 before ECE 264B).

groups.rule: Both required (4 units each).

groups.name: Additional Units
groups.rule: Select 16 units — at least one course from EACH of the four sections (I: 255 series; II: 257A/257C; III: 258 series; IV: 259 series & 257B).

groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH, PHYS or COGS (letter grade). Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

Recommended: ECE 278, SIO 203A-B / MAE 294A-B, and remaining SIO 210/240/260/280. Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH or PHYS (letter grade). Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: ECE 201 is required; choose 2 more courses from the list (12 units total). ECE 247A-B-C is cross-listed with BENG/NANO 247A-B-C — credit only once.

groups.rule: 16 units: three CORE graduate (200+) courses from ONE ECE area (AEM, AOS, AP-EDM, CTS, CE, ECS, ISRC, MI, MLDS, NDS, PHO or SIP) PLUS one graduate or upper-division course in a biology-, biochemistry- or medicine-focused area (eligibility is by content, not department).

groups.rule: Any 4-unit, 200+ course from ECE, CSE, MAE, BENG, CENG, NANO, SE, MATS, MATH, PHYS or COGS (letter grade). Up to 12 units of upper-division ECE (111+) OR two upper-div ECE plus one CSE (100+) may count.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive
plans.note: 12 courses (6 core + 6 electives), all for letter grade; pass comprehensive exam; no research.

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 36 units coursework (6 core + 3 electives) + 12 units research (BENG 299, S/U); thesis.

groups.rule: Choose 3 courses (12 units) from the Engineering Physics core list, taken for a letter grade.

courses.title: Bioinformatics II: Introduction to Bioinformatics Algorithms

Systems Biology and Bioengineering I: Biological Components

courses.title: Systems Medicine: Data Analytics and Mechanisms

groups.name: Electives
groups.rule: Plan II: 6 elective courses (24 units); Plan I: 3 elective courses (12 units). Graduate-level BENG courses, plus graduate engineering/science courses in other departments with faculty approval; letter grade. Up to two undergraduate prerequisite courses (≤8 units) may count with adviser permission. BENG 281/282/299/501 units may not apply.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive
plans.note: 20 core + 4 electives (16 units); ≤8 units NANO 298 may apply. Comprehensive exam (60% min per component, one retake).

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 20 core + 1 elective (4 units) + 12 units NANO 299. Thesis with 3-member committee.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: Five required core courses (20 units), all letter grade.

groups.name: Electives
groups.rule: Plan II: four electives (16 units) from any focus area, incl. up to 8 units NANO 298. Plan I: one elective (4 units) plus 12 units NANO 299 research. NANO 200 required every quarter but does not count toward the 36 units.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive
plans.note: 20 core + 4 electives (16 units), CENG or STEM grad/upper-div; ≤8 units CENG 297 may apply. Comprehensive exam.

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 20 core + 1 elective (4 units) + 12 units CENG 299. Letter grade, C- or above passing.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: Five required core courses (20 units), all letter grade.

groups.name: Electives
groups.rule: Plan II: four electives (16 units) of CENG or STEM grad/upper-div courses, incl. up to 8 units CENG 297. Plan I: one elective (4 units) plus 12 units CENG 299 research. CENG 205 required every quarter but does not count toward the 36 units.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive
plans.note: Comprehensive exam covering the core courses; no thesis. Pass/No Pass (60%+).

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: Thesis research with faculty advisor; requires 12 units MATS 299, thesis, and oral defense.

groups.name: Required Core
groups.rule: Complete all four Mandatory Core Courses: MATS 201A, 201B, 201C, and 227.

courses.title: Solid State Diffusion and Reaction Kinetics

groups.rule: Select at least two of the Elective Core Courses (min. 8 units).

courses.title: Electronic and Photonic Properties of Materials

Mechanical Behavior of Polymers and Composites
Engineering Solar Cells at the Nanoscale

groups.name: Electives
groups.rule: Remaining units from graduate-level (200+) courses to reach 36 total; letter grades (P/NP only for MATS 200 and 299). Plan I typically fills these with MATS 299 research (up to 12 units).

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive Exam, Standard
plans.note: 12 breadth + 12 depth + 16 electives/research; pass comprehensive exams. Max 4 units research (CSE 293) count toward electives.

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 12 breadth + 12 depth + 16 electives/research; requires 8–12 units CSE 298 (Independent Research) plus a thesis.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive Exam, Interdisciplinary
plans.note: 48 units: 12 breadth + 12 depth + 12 CSE electives/research + 12 units approved coursework in a secondary field.

groups.name: Breadth
groups.rule: One graduate course (4 units) from EACH of the three breadth areas — Theory, Systems, and Applications; letter grade, B- or higher. Full official code lists: Theory: CSE 200, 201A, 202, 203A, 203B, 205A, 207A. Systems: CSE 220, 221, 222A, 223B, 224, 231, 234, 237A-D, 240A-B, 241A, 243A, 244A. Applications: CSE 210, 216, 218, 230, 232, 250A, 251A, 251U, 252A-B, 256, 260, 280A, 284.

courses.title: Introduction to Synthesis Methodologies in VLSI CAD
Principles of Artificial Intelligence: Probabilistic Reasoning and Learning

groups.name: Depth
groups.rule: Three graduate courses (12 units) from ONE depth area, letter grade. Depth areas: AI, Bioinformatics, Computer Engineering, Computer Systems (& Security), Database Systems, Graphics & Vision, Human-Computer Interaction, Programming Languages/Compilers/SE, Robotics, Theoretical CS. Computer Engineering majors must use Computer Engineering as depth.

groups.name: Electives and Research
groups.rule: 16 units electives/research (12 for the Interdisciplinary option, plus 12 in the secondary field). Letter grade. Max one CSE upper-division undergrad course. Max 4 units research (CSE 293). Plan I: 8–12 units CSE 298 fulfill this in place of electives.

plans.name: Plan II — Comprehensive
plans.note: 12 courses (48 units): 2 core + 6 focus-area (two 3-course sequences) + 4 technical electives. Written comprehensive exam. Also 3 quarters of SE 290 (seminar).

plans.name: Plan I — Thesis
plans.note: 9 courses (36 units): 2 core + 6 focus-area + 3 electives, plus 12 units SE 299 (thesis). Requires research advisor + defense. Also 3 quarters SE 290.

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: Complete 2 of these 7 core courses (SE 233 or SE 276A may count, but not both): SE 201A, 202, 203A, 241, 271, 233, 276A.

courses.title: Solid Mechanics for Structural and Aerospace Engineering
Finite Element Methods in Solid Mechanics I

groups.name: Focus-area Courses
groups.rule: Two focus-area sequences (each = three preapproved courses from one area), i.e. 6 courses / 24 units. Choose 2 of 8 areas: Structural Analysis; Computational Mechanics & Finite Elements; Structural Design; Earthquake Engineering; Advanced Composites; Geotechnical Engineering; Solid Mechanics; Structural Health Monitoring & NDE. (See official page for each area's course list.)

groups.name: Electives
groups.rule: Technical electives: any science/math/engineering graduate course, or approved upper-division. Plan II = 4 (16 units); Plan I = 3 (12 units). ≤4 units SE 296 may apply.

plans.name: Ph.D. Coursework
plans.note: PhD-only program; MS not offered. Coursework portion only (16 core + 16 elective = 32 units). Two tracks: BISB and Biomedical Informatics (BMI).

groups.name: Core Courses
groups.rule: Four core courses (16 units). Shared: BENG 202/CSE 282 and MATH 283. Track third course: BISB → BENG 203/CSE 283; BMI → MED 264. Plus a Fourth Core (4 units) from CSE 280A, CSE 284, ECE 208, ECE 213, BNFO 285, BNFO 286 (or BENG 203 if not used). Pick the four for your track.

courses.title: Genomics, Proteomics, and Network Biology (BISB track core)
Principles of Biomedical Informatics (BMI track core)

groups.name: Track Electives
groups.rule: 16 units of electives. BISB track: ≥4 units CS series + ≥4 units BIO series. BMI track: ≥4 units CS series + ≥8 units BMI series. Remaining from any listed category.

courses.title: Structure of Biomolecules and Biomolecular Assemblies
Systems Biology and Bioengineering II: Large-Scale Data Analysis
Modeling Clinical Data and Knowledge for Computation
Introduction to Biomedical Natural Language Processing
